pages.raw_text:
We notice with regret the report
of the arrest of one of our former
supreme court judges upon a
charge of embezzlement. At the
same time we read that the Great
Northern Railway Co., which took
Judge Gordon off the bench to
serve that corporation, stated
through w. Hill, the head of the
company, that no such embezzle-
ment has taken place. Mr.. Gordon
was one of the lights both of the
bench and the bar of this state,
whose abilities and work for the
corporation might easily be worth
$4,000 at the time when the rail-
ways were in hot water with the
public as regards the mutual regu-
lations of rates and the application
of the law to the great systems of
travel and freight rates, and if Mr.,
Gordon had the right to apply the
company's drafts to whatever pur-
pose was the most pressing, &C
cording to the admission of Mr..
Hill that the accounts are all
right, what right nave our authorl-
ties to step in merely for the pur-
pose of taking up case of &C
counting as between Mr.. Gordon
and his company? 1t seems to
SAVIOR of desire to make a scape-
goat of Gordon to appease a public
clamor founded upon some persons
statements who are enemies of our
former Judge.


Nothing venal appeared in the
conduct of Judge Gordon and
Judge Root, and why should the
county of Spokane be put to great
expense merely owing to the form
of drawing money adopted by the
Great Northern Railway Co.?
James Finlay.
